old_claim.note: E-Commerce Platforms provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: CRM software took shape by 1993, before the scoped Amazon and eBay Web-platform milestone of 1995.

source_shape.source_locator: The Library of Congress timeline states that Amazon and eBay both came online in 1995 and describes the ensuing wave of Web e-commerce platforms.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Library of Congress chronology dates Amazon and eBay Web commerce to 1995.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The existing CRM chronology predates the directly sourced Web-platform milestone, so e-commerce is a later customer channel rather than an origin prerequisite.

ontology_before: Early CRM software depended on Web e-commerce platforms that appeared later.
ontology_after: CRM remains based on enterprise data and software, with Web commerce available as a later integration.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-launch-source-check-completion: customer_relationship_management no longer directly depends on e_commerce_platforms.
would_reject_if: The CRM node is rescoped exclusively to e-commerce customer-management modules after 1995.
A reliable source moves the scoped Web-platform milestone before the CRM date.
short_reason: CRM software took shape by 1993, before the scoped Amazon and eBay Web-platform milestone of 1995.
